creator: swills@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Mon, Mar 17, 2014 at 09:10:01AM -0700, Joseph Benden wrote:
> It looks like we will have to use my more complicated build scripts. The
> gem available from RG is a "filler" saying that the gem is no longer
> updated with RG and that you should install using packages available at
> http://vagrantup.com - which of course there is no FreeBSD package.
> 
> I'm recommending we use my more complex build files. Thoughts?

Ok. But using the source from github doesn't mean we can't use the GEM stuff in
ports, I think. I'll have to look at that more closely. If not, no big deal.

> PS: It was pointed out on the vagrant mailing list that versions 1.4 or
> higher require Ruby 2.0 or newer. So yes, we'd have to depend on Ruby 2.1.

This is the bigger issue. Technically, right now, we only have gem19. If you
specify ruby 2.0 or 2.1, that's fine, but you don't get a GEMBIN to go with it.
You only get that for the default ruby.

This is what failed for me in my initial test of this using poudriere. This is
deficiency in ports, but it's one we'll have to deal with, either by fixing
ports or some change to the vagrant port.

I suppose one ugly way would be to IGNORE if the default ruby version wasn't
2.0 or 2.1. This way the port would be useful for those who build their own
packages with default ruby set to 2.0 or 2.1, but others wouldn't get an error
and we wouldn't get errors from official package building. Not ideal, but at
least gets it in.

Steve
